title: Cookies, Credential Modes, API Keys, Bearer Tokens, and Secret Hygiene · Go Client Systems · Systems Transfer
summary: A backup automation team integrating an unfamiliar artifact service must satisfy changed endpoint, payload, authority, byte, and time constraints without a recipe for cookies, credential modes, api keys, bearer tokens, and secret hygiene, producing least-authority credential and session policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a Go 1.26 net/http least-authority credential and session policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through predic…
objectives: Apply cookie domain, host-only, path, expiry, Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite scope through a deliberate jar policy. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Recognize ambient cookie authority and require CSRF defenses for state-changing browser requests. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Place API keys in approved headers or signing inputs and exclude them from URLs, source, telemetry, and error text. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Cookie Jar Scope
steps.instruction:
lab case — Go backup artifact client handles host-only session cookie SID-2, SameSite policy, API key, and expiring bearer token while two accounts run concurrently. Define jar isolation, CSRF, scope, rotation, redaction, and redirect boundaries.

For Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Cookie Jar Scope, keep Cookie Jar Scope connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
